title: Legacy (2006) 4
content:

'''''Legacy'' 4''' is the fourth issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' comic series.

==Publisher's summary==
''A galaxy-shaking secret is revealed in the heat of battle! Old friendships will be strained to the breaking point, unlikely new alliances will be formed, and the relentless forces of evil will pursue their dark ends with even greater vigor and purpose. In a universe on the brink of either destruction or salvation, it is the actions of an unexpected few that will determine the fates of all. The previously uncharted future of ''Star Wars'' takes a surprising turn in this latest issue of ''Legacy''—be there!''

==Plot summary==
At the Imperial Base on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]], also serving as the Headquarters for the 407th Stormtrooper Division, [[Anson Trask]] attempts to find his squad, and has a run in with [[Darth Maleval]]. [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Ran Harkas]] steps in and saves the "[[Noob]]" from the Sith's wrath and takes him to Joker Squad, Company C, Battalion 9. There, Anson meets [[Jes Gistang]], [[Hondo Karr]] and [[Vax Potorr]]. The troopers welcome him by inviting him to join the bet they have on how long he will live. [[Gil Cassel|Lieutenant Gil Cassel]] informs Joker Squad that [[Morlish Veed|Grand Admiral Veed]] and the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]] have ordered the termination of the 908th division on [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] for defecting from the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|New Empire]] and joining the former Emperor [[Roan Fel]] and that it is Joker Squad's job to see it done.

On Borosk, Joker Squad is assigned to capture and secure the gun and shield placements along the perimeter wall of the 908th's Headquarters. After they secure the gun platform the troop is allowed to rest for the night. Anson and Jes talk about their reasons for signing up with The Empire and Jes goes on to tell the brief back stories of the other members in Joker Squad. Lieutenant Cassel is confronted by Darth Maleval who tells him that his idea of a truce with the 908th is out of the question and that an example will be made of the 908th. Hondo comes up behind Darth Maleval with a drawn blade and is subsequently Force thrown in a wall. As Maleval walks away, talk of joining the 908th or simply walking away from the fight arise but Sergeant Harkas reminds them that a soldiers duty is to complete the mission no matter the cost. One by one the members of Joker Squad agree.

The next day the squad breaches the main fortress of the 908th Headquarters and press into the main mess hall, were Vex steps on a mine and is killed. Jes is killed when enemy fire hits one of the power packs on her waist and Sergeant Harkas is severely wounded by the ensuing explosion. When the battle is over, Darth Maleval brings forth a bound 908th prisoner, Captain Jared Cassel, Lieutenant Cassel's brother and orders Lieutenant Cassel to execute his own brother. The Lieutenant refuses and as Darth Maleval raises his lightsaber to kill both men, Hondo charges the Sith's back with his sword. Darth Maleval back hands Hondo with a clenched fist before lassoing Hondo's neck with a shock whip and electrocuting him. Anson shoots Darth Maleval in the back killing the Sith and saving Hondo. It is reported that Darth Maleval and Hondo Karr were killed in battle. Sergeant Harkas is taken away by a med-droid leaving Anson the only remaining member of Joker Squad. Anson is approached by a "Newbie" looking for Joker Squad to which he replies, "I'm it." The two troopers walk away to find their transport ships.

==Development==
{{Quote|I'll just add that NO ONE working on the book is IGNORING the deadlines. We're working hard to do quality work AND hit the deadline. That's why, from tome ''[sic]'' to time, we'll have fill-ins like issue 4. Except that I'm really working at using those stories to expand on the LEGACY era. You'll see what I mean as we go.|John Ostrander|<ref name="Ostrander fillin" />}}
In [[June]] [[2006]], [[Dark Horse Comics]] began publishing the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]''.<ref name="Legacy 0">[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]]</ref> Its initial story arc, [[Star Wars: Legacy: Broken|''Broken'']], was originally intended to span ''Legacy''{{'s}} first six issues, with series architects [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]] providing the script and the pencils, respectively. ''Noob'', a standalone issue drawn by a new penciller, would follow ''Broken'' in order to give Duursema breathing room with ''Legacy''{{'s}} monthly schedule. When the design work involved with the series' launch and the demands of plotting out a new story with new characters proved more work than the creative team expected, ''Noob'' was moved up to become ''Legacy''{{'s}} fourth issue and an interlude to ''Broken''.<ref name="Ostrander interlude">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25349912|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #4: Noob|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-10-16|time=11:09 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193212/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-4-noob.24857432/page-8}}</ref>

Ostrander and Duursema chose to make ''Noob'' the series' fourth issue because they felt that the end of [[Legacy (2006) 3|''Broken, Part 3'']] was the most natural break in ''Broken''{{'s}} story—they believed that interrupting the arc after its [[Legacy (2006) 5|fourth]] or [[Legacy (2006) 6|fifth issue]] would have been more disruptive to its flow.<ref name="Ostrander interlude" /> Although ''Legacy''{{'s}} fourth issue was originally solicited as ''Broken, Part 4'', with a projected release date of [[September 6]],<ref name="original solicit">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24091060|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=wild_karrde|date=2006-06-09|time=12:28 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192558/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060}}</ref> Dark Horse's official website later modified it into a solicitation for ''Noob'',<ref name="solicitation correction">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24775945|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=wild_karrde|date=2006-08-23|time=1:24 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192558/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060}}</ref> and ''Broken, Part 4'' became ''Legacy''{{'s}} fifth issue.<ref name="Randy weighs in">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24832302|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=[[Randy Stradley|Randy_Stradley]]|date=2006-08-28|time=12:25 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192609/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060/page-4}}</ref> However, ''Noob''{{'s}} profile on DarkHorse.com has always mistakenly listed both the credits and publisher's summary of ''Broken, Part 4''.<ref name="Dark Horse profile" /><ref name="Dark Horse profile Legacy 5">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/13-457/Star-Wars-Legacy-5|text=Star Wars: Legacy #5}}</ref> Duursema has acknowledged the mistake.<ref name="Duursema mistake">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25358104|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #4: Noob|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2006-09-05|time=7:13 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193212/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-4-noob.24857432/page-8}}</ref>

Although [[Travel Foreman]] filled in as ''Noob''{{'s}} artist, providing both penciling and inking, Ostrander—with input from Duursema—wrote its script, as he wanted to maintain some control over ''Legacy'' so soon after its launch.<ref name="Ostrander control">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25358104|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #4: Noob|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-10-17|time=7:03 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193633/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-4-noob.24857432/page-8}}</ref> He referred to ''Noob'' as a "fill-in" issue but assured fans that he was working hard to use its story to expand on the [[Legacy era]].<ref name="Ostrander fillin">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24850014|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-08-29|time=9:36 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192518/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060/page-6}}</ref> [[Brad Anderson]] colored the issue while [[Michael David Thomas]] provided lettering, and both Anderson and Duursema drew the issue's cover.<ref name="issue" /> Although ''Noob'' is not part of the ''Broken'' story arc, its cover follows the pattern<ref name="issue" /> used by artist [[Adam Hughes]] on the covers of the first five issues of ''Broken'', showcasing three new characters against a bluish-gray background.<ref name="Legacy 1">[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]]</ref><ref name="Legacy 2">[[Legacy (2006) 2|''Legacy'' (2006) 2]]</ref><ref name="Legacy 6">[[Legacy (2006) 6|''Legacy'' (2006) 6]]</ref>

''Noob''{{'s}} title refers to its protagonist, Anson Trask, who is fresh out of his stormtrooper training.<ref name="issue" /> Ostrander recalled the term from his days with the [[Wikipedia:Boy Scouts of America|Boy Scouts of America]],<ref name="Ostrander boy scout">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25428549|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #4: Noob|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-10-25|time=7:36 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193536/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-4-noob.24857432/page-11}}</ref> when it was shorthand for "newbie" or "rookie."<ref name="Ostrander DH boards noob">{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?p=137905#p137905|thread=LEGACY #4 -- "Noob! Hey, Noob! Nice haircut!"|user=[[John Ostrander|johnostrander]]|date=2006-10-14|time=20:18|nobackup=1}}</ref> He was unaware of its use in online circles<ref name="Ostrander boy scout" /> and later stated that if he had been, he likely would not have used it, as he felt that it had the potential to draw attention to itself and distract readers from the story.<ref name="Ostrander DH boards noob" /> [[Randy Stradley]], the issue's editor, joked on the [[Jedi Council Forums]] that "noob" had been a replacement after [[Lucas Licensing]] had forbidden them from using "[[Wikipedia:FNG syndrome|FNG]]."<ref name="Randy FNG">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25320626|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #4: Noob|user=[[Randy Stradley|Randy_Stradley]]|date=2006-10-13|time=8:24 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193116/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-4-noob.24857432/page-5}}</ref> When designing Darth Maleval, Ostrander drew inspiration from the title of a novel by [[Wikipedia:France|French]] writer [[Wikipedia:Pierre Boulle|Pierre Boulle]], as he liked the sound of "Maleval" and thought that it would work well in a science fiction setting.<ref name="Maleval inspiration">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24778075|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-08-23|time=4:02 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200830192401/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060/page-2}}</ref> In ''Noob''{{'s}} script, Ostrander described Lieutenant Gil Cassel as being his late twenties, but Foreman's art depicts him as an older man.<ref name="Gil's age 1">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25428559|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #4: Noob|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander]]|date=2006-10-25|time=7:38 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193536/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-4-noob.24857432/page-11}}</ref> Stradley claimed responsibility for the discrepancy, stating that he would have made adjustments after receiving the art if time had permitted.<ref name="Gil's age 2">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25429683|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #4: Noob|user=[[Randy Stradley|Randy_Stradley]]|date=2006-10-25|time=10:50 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193536/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-4-noob.24857432/page-11}}</ref>

With ''Noob'' scheduled for a September 6 release,<ref name="original solicit" /> a four-page preview was published on DarkHorse.com in late August.<ref name="Noob preview">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24775945|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=wild_karrde|date=2006-08-23|time=1:24 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192558/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060}}</ref> However, the issue was soon afterward pushed back to [[October 4]]<ref name="Noob delay">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24784705|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=wild_karrde|date=2006-08-24|time=5:25 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192558/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060}}</ref> due to compounded delays from the previous issues of ''Broken''.<ref name="Issue 3 original release date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23831167|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #3: Broken, part 3 (of 6)|user=Vengeance1003|date=2006-05-07|time=8:56 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192400/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-3-broken-part-3-of-6.23831167}} {{C|Issue 3's originally scheduled release date}}</ref><ref name="Issue 3 final release date">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/13-455/Star-Wars-Legacy-3|text=Star Wars: Legacy #3}} {{C|Issue 3's ultimate release date}}</ref> Duursema apologized for the delays, citing the unforeseen difficulties involved with exploring new territory.<ref name="Duursema apology">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24695829|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #3: Broken, part 3 (of 6)|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2006-08-16|time=10:36 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192224/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-3-broken-part-3-of-6.23831167/page-6}}</ref> ''Noob'' was eventually [[Wikipedia:Direct market|released]] to comic shops on [[October 11]]. With advertisements, the issue is thirty-two pages long;<ref name="Dark Horse profile" /> the issue contains twenty-two pages of story.<ref name="issue" />

''Noob'', along with issues eight through thirteen of ''Legacy'', was later included in the trade paperback ''[[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 2: Shards]]'', which was released on [[March 26]], [[2008]].<ref name="Shards profile">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/14-990/Star-Wars-Legacy-Volume-2--Shards-TPB|text=Star Wars: Legacy Volume 2--Shards TPB}}</ref> ''Noob'' is the second issue in the collection, coming after the chronologically-earlier [[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]].<ref name="Shards">''[[Star Wars: Legacy Volume 2: Shards]]''</ref> The same ordering is used in the hardcover omnibus ''[[Star Wars: Legacy Book 1]]'',<ref name="Legacy Book 1 hardcover">''[[Star Wars: Legacy Book 1]]''</ref> released on [[June 5]], [[2013]].<ref name="Legacy Book 1 hardcover Dark Horse profile">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/23-428/Star-Wars-Legacy-Book-1-HC|text=Star Wars: Legacy Book 1 HC}}</ref> Within the hardcover's pages, both ''Allies'' and ''Noob'' follow ''Broken''.<ref name="Legacy Book 1 hardcover" /> ''Noob'' was additionally included in the [[Wikipedia:Polish language|Polish language]] omnibus ''[[Star Wars Komiks 2/2008]]'', published on [[October 17]], [[2008]]; also included were the ''[[Star Wars]]'' comics "[[Moment of Doubt]]," "[[The Way of the Warrior (comic story)|The Way of the Warrior]]," and sixteen pages of [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]]. ''Noob''{{'s}} cover art was used on the front of the book.<ref name="Polish Komiks">[[Star Wars Komiks 2/2008|''Star Wars Komiks'' 2/2008]]</ref> Dark Horse released ''Noob'' digitally on [[August 3]], [[2011]],<ref name="Digital Release" /> and the issue was later included along with the rest of ''Legacy'' and the miniseries [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']] in the digital "Star Wars: Legacy XL Bundle," released on [[February 12]], [[2014]].<ref name="Digital bundle">{{DarkHorse|subdomain=digital|url=profile/4396.star-wars-legacy-xl-bundle|text=Star Wars: Legacy XL Bundle}}</ref>

==Release and reception==
34,346 copies of ''Legacy 4'' were shipped to comic shops for the issue's release in October, making it Dark Horse' best-selling comic of the month and the fiftiest best-selling comic overall.<ref name="Comichron">{{WebCite|url=http://www.comichron.com/monthlycomicssales/2006/2006-10.html|text=October 2006 Comic Book Sales Figures|author=[[John Jackson Miller]]|work=Comichron: The Comics Chronicles|archivedate=20140401221504}}</ref>

==Continuity==
[[File:NoobMeetsGunGirl-SWL04.png|thumb|right|200px|Jes Gistang was the first named female stormtrooper to appear in ''Star Wars'' canon.]]
{{Quote|I went back and forth with two pieces of reference I had on the Quarren. Since the artist for the issue had designed his Quarren around the 5 fingered Quarren, I went with that for consistency. I prefer the three fingered Quarren myself… by 3 or 5 fingers, Maleval is still a nasty lookin Sith!|Jan Duursema, on designing ''Noob''{{'s}} cover|<ref name="Duursema three fingers" />}}
Prior to ''Noob''{{'s}} release, its events were indirectly referenced in ''Broken, Part 3'' by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Oron Jaeger]] of the Empire-in-exile. The issue sees Roan Fel [[Fall of Bastion|retake]] the [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] from Krayt's Imperials, thanks in part to Jaeger having slowly rotated Fel-loyal stormtroopers into service on the planet. When Fel asks Jaeger if all of the troops have arrived, the general responds "The 908th has been delayed."<ref name="Legacy 3">[[Legacy (2006) 3|''Legacy'' (2006) 3]]</ref>

When designing ''Noob''{{'s}} cover, Duursema had two different pieces of reference art for Maleval's species, the Quarren—one depicted them with three-fingered hands, and the other with five-fingered hands. Although she preferred the former look, she ultimately chose to go for consistency with the five-fingered look that Travel Foreman was using on the interior art. Maleval thus has five fingers both in and on the front of the issue.<ref name="Duursema three fingers">{{JCFCite|url=posts/24849303|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2006-08-29|time=8:16 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192518/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/legacy-5-broken-part-4-of-6.24091060/page-6}}</ref>

Jes Gistang's appearance in ''Noob'' made her the first named [[Gender/Legends|female]] stormtrooper in ''Star Wars'' [[canon]],<ref name="issue" /> although an image of an unnamed female trooper had earlier been included in the preview issue [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]],<ref name="Legacy 0" /> and ''Star Wars'' creator [[George Lucas]] had established the existence of female stormtroopers while preparing background material for [[1977]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|original ''Star Wars'' film]].<ref name="Imperial Icons">{{InsiderCite|148|Imperial Icons}}</ref> Gistang's weapon was first given a name in [[2009]]'s ''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'': the BB-23 heavy blaster cannon. The guide asserts that the cannon's power pack must be replaced after ten shots,<ref name="LECG 182">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'', p. 182</ref> which contrasts with ''Noob''{{'s}} claim that a new power pack is needed after just four shots.<ref name="issue" />

==Legacy==
{{Quote|Standing at a bold 1/6th scale and roughly 12" tall, this rarely seen but often asked for character from the vast expanded ''Star Wars'' universe is one to watch.|Gentle Giant Studios describes its statue of Jes Gistang|<ref name="GentleGiant">{{GentleGiantCite|set=Gentle Giant, Ltd.|pack=Jes Gistang Limited Edition Statue||altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/story/other/Gentle_Giant_Female_Stormtrooper_Statue_137588.asp}}</ref>}}
[[File:Karrjoinsthejokers.jpg|thumb|left|300px|''Legacy''{{'s}} forty-first issue reveals how Hondo Karr joined Joker Squad.]]
Anson Trask, Ran Harkas, and a newly-staffed Joker Squad go on to play minor roles in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 16|sixteenth]], [[Legacy (2006) 17|seventeenth]], and [[Legacy (2006) 48|forty-eighth]] issues,<ref name="Legacy 16">[[Legacy (2006) 16|''Legacy'' (2006) 16]]</ref><ref name="Legacy 17">[[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]]</ref><ref name="Legacy 48">[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]]</ref> as well as issues [[Legacy — War 3|three]] and [[Legacy — War 4|four]] of the followup miniseries ''Star Wars: Legacy—War''.<ref name="Legacy War 3">[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]]</ref><ref name="Legacy War 4">[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]]</ref> Hondo Karr reappears in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 20|twentieth]] and [[Legacy (2006) 21|twenty-first]] issues as a new recruit in the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]]'s [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]],<ref name="Legacy 20">[[Legacy (2006) 20|''Legacy'' (2006) 20]]</ref><ref name="Legacy 21">[[Legacy (2006) 21|''Legacy'' (2006) 21]]</ref> but he deserts his post in the series' [[Legacy (2006) 41|forty-first issue]], which also reveals through flashbacks how he first joined the Jokers.<ref name="Legacy 41">[[Legacy (2006) 41|''Legacy'' (2006) 41]]</ref> The word "noob" appears again in [[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]], spoken by Captain [[Gunn Yage]] of Darth Krayt's Empire.<ref name="Legacy 14">[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]]</ref>

Various characters and organizations from ''Noob'' were given entries in 2008's ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'';<ref name="CSWE Gistang">{{CSWECite|I|342-323|Gistang, Jes}}</ref><ref name="CSWE Harkas">{{CSWECite|II|19|Harkas, Ran}}</ref><ref name="CSWE Jokers">{{CSWECite|II|170|Joker Squad}}</ref><ref name="CSWE Karr">{{CSWECite|II|200|Karr, Hondo}}</ref><ref name="CSWE Trask">{{CSWECite|III|260|Trask, Anson}}</ref> and Hondo Karr, Darth Maleval, and Joker Squad are profiled and expanded upon in 2009's ''Legacy Era Campaign Guide''.<ref name="LECG Karr">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' {{C|under "Hondo Karr," p. 137-138}}</ref><ref name="LECG Maleval">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' {{C|under "Darth Maleval," p. 171}}</ref><ref name="LECG Jokers 1">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' {{C|under "Joker Squad," p. 160}}</ref><ref name="LECG Jokers 2">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' {{C|under "Joker Squad Stormtroopers," p. 163-165}}</ref> Karr's entry confirms his [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] heritage,<ref name="LECG Karr" /> which is only hinted at in ''Noob''.<ref name="issue" /> Both the Jokers and Jes Gistang specifically are mentioned in ''[[Imperial Icons]]'', an article from 2014's [[Star Wars Insider 148|''Star Wars Insider'' 148]]. The Jokers are cited as a notable stormtrooper unit of the ''Star Wars'' [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], while Gistang is named as one of the "best-known members of the rarely-explored ranks of female stormtroopers."<ref name="Imperial Icons" />

In 2009, [[Hasbro]] released a set of Joker Squad action figures as part of its ''[[Star Wars: The Legacy Collection]]'' line. The set includes six figures—Trask, Harkas, Gistang, Karr, Potor, and Darth Maleval. Each figure comes packaged with at least one weapon that appears in ''Noob'': Maleval with his lightsaber and shock whip, Gistang with her heavy blaster cannon, Karr with his knife, and each stormtrooper with a blaster rifle. Advertised with the tagline "As seen in Dark Horse Comics ''Star Wars: Legacy'' No. 4," the set's packaging includes a brief summary of the events of ''Noob''.<ref name="Hasbro set">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|pack=Joker Squad|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TLCExJokerSquad.asp}}</ref> It was sold exclusively through the online retailer Entertainment Earth.<ref name="Hasbro set" /><ref name="Entertainment Earth">{{WebCite|url=http://www.entertainmentearth.com/prodinfo.asp?number=HS87923|text=Star Wars Joker Squad Stormtrooper Figures - An EE Exclusive|work=Entertainment Earth|archivedate=20100508222917}}</ref> 2011 saw [[Gentle Giant Studios]] release a 1/6 scale statue of Jes Gistang, referring to her as a "rarely seen but often asked for character."<ref name="Gentle Giant">{{GentleGiantCite|link=product_p/80094.htm|set=Gentle Giant, Ltd.|pack=Jes Gistang Limited Edition Statue}}</ref>

==Media==
{{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Legacy (2006) 4}}
===Cover gallery===
